etymology.description: 行(行) (xíng, 'to walk; to go') refers to movement or the act of proceeding and 動(動) (dòng, 'to move') implies motion or activity. Together, 行動(行動) (xíngdòng, 'action; movement') conveys the idea of initiating or engaging in a movement or activity.
etymology.interpretation: 行(行) (xíng, 'to walk; to go') suggests moving forward or engaging in an activity, while 動(動) (dòng, 'to move') emphasizes physical or dynamic motion. The two together form the concept of 'action', stressing the start of movement or the execution of a plan as a coordinated or purposeful shift, akin to making progress or performing an act.

parts.english: to walk; to go; behavior; conduct
to move; to act; touch; use

examples.chinese: 他的行動迅速(他的行動迅速)。
如果没有行動(如果沒有行動)，就不会有结果(就不會有結果)。
examples.pinyin: Rúguǒ méiyǒu xíngdòng, jiù bú huì yǒu jiéguǒ.
examples.english: Without action, there will be no result.

examples.chinese: 我们需要采取行動(我們需要採取行動)，保护环境(保護環境)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen xūyào cǎiqǔ xíngdòng, bǎohù huánjìng.
examples.english: We need to take action to protect the environment.
